description_clean:
This is a great antique Persian Serapi. These rugs also known as an antique Heriz,
because they were woven in the city of Heriz before the transition to busier patterns
and brighter colors. This rug has a very stately design with a characteristic ivory medallion
on a very soft open rust field with blue spandrels and an ivory border. It is 9x11, circa 1890

description_clean: This is a Persian Sultanabad or otherwise known as an early Mahal. Sultanabad’s are known for their all over motif of flowers, vines, and tendrils. This particular rug is very unusual in that it has so many colors; blues, greens, and even yellow. It also has an ivory border so that it has a very light over-all appearance. Circa 1900. 8-10x11-10.

description_clean: Circa 1900, 6-1x11-2, This Kurdish rug from northwest Persia features a striking ivory medallion set against a rich navy-blue field. The border is a colorful floral motif. This rug showcases the distinctive craftsmanship and bold designs characteristic of Kurdish weaving traditions.
description_raw:
15428
Circa 1900, 6-1x11-2, This Kurdish rug from northwest Persia features a striking ivory medallion set against a rich navy-blue field. The border is a colorful floral motif. This rug showcases the distinctive craftsmanship and bold designs characteristic of Kurdish weaving traditions.

description_clean: Circa 1900, 6x10-5, This Kurdish rug from northwest Persia features a central field with a repeating pattern of elaborate vase motifs, each symmetrically arranged along the central axis. The vases are depicted with intricate floral decorations and vine elements, which add a sense of harmony and natural beauty to the design. The motif is on a soft cream field. The rich red border is adorned with traditional Kurdish designs, adding depth and contrast. The inscription on the rug is in Persian (Farsi) and is likely a signature or a poetic verse.

description_clean: Circa 1910, 2-11x14-5, This Persian Karaja runner showcases a series of intricate medallions in rich reds and blues, connected by geometric motifs along a deep navy field. The detailed designs and earth-toned colors reflect the traditional craftsmanship and style characteristic of early Karaja rugs.

description_clean: Circa 1900, 3-8x6-1, This Caucasian Karagashli rug features a striking design with three large diamond-shaped medallions running down the center, each filled with intricate geometric patterns. The background is a deep navy blue, highlighted by intricate motifs in white, green, and light blue. The borders are densely packed with zigzag and floral patterns, framed by a series of smaller, detailed bands. The combination of bold colors and detailed patterns showcases the traditional craftsmanship and distinctive style of Karagashli rugs.
description_raw:
15449
Circa 1900, 3-8x6-1, This Caucasian Karagashli rug features a striking design with three large diamond-shaped medallions running down the center, each filled with intricate geometric patterns. The background is a deep navy blue, highlighted by intricate motifs in white, green, and light blue. The borders are densely packed with zigzag and floral patterns, framed by a series of smaller, detailed bands. The combination of bold colors and detailed patterns showcases the traditional craftsmanship and distinctive style of Karagashli rugs.

description_clean: Circa 1900, 3-1x4-4, This Caucasian Perpedil rug, originating from the Kuba region of the Caucasus, has a classic rams horn motif on a navy-blue field. The rams horn motif, often stylized in various geometric forms, is a symbol of strength. The main border is flanked by several smaller guard borders, each with its own set of geometric patterns.
